titleStmt.title: SII 24.573: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswamy
No. 573.

summary.p: States that this (the gold pot) which weighs 12,515 varāha and was stolen by thieves, was recovered, re-done and restored to the temple by Raṅgappa oḍeya. When it was stolen in the year Vikṛiti, on the 7th day of the dark fort-, night of Bhādrapada, during the reign of Vijayaraṅga Chokkanātha-nāyaka son of Viśvanātha-nāyaka, Tupākula Rāmakṛishṇappa-nāyuḍu recovered and restored it to the temple again. The record is not dated.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
samukhaṁ
śrīraṁganāyakulaku raṁgappayyagāri vubhayaṁ
panne reṁḍuvēlu nnēnūṭapadihēnu varāla yettu yī biṁde yevarainā Āśiṁci yettitē gaṁggalō kapiladhēnuvupanu caṁppina pāpāna pōgalavāru
cōrakulu yettukonirāgā pāḷenai.e. pāśyamuna; pāḷyaṁ stands for ūḍaiyārpāḷayam.kanipeṭṭi raṁggappa kāḷākatōḷa voḍayāri vāru maḷlinni lessacēyiṁcci sannidhiki Aṁppina biṁde
vikṛtināma saṁvatsara bhādrapada ba 7lu śrīmadviśvanāthanāyanivijayaraṁga cokkanāthanāyanivāri dināla śrīraṁgganāyakula baṁgārubiṁde
cōrakulu koṁccupōyi vuṁḍḍaṁgā tupākula rāmakṛṣṇappanāyanivāru teppiṁci sama¨rppiṁcināru
Digital edition of SII 24.573 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
